prompts.proposer:
Call proposer_brief() FIRST. The tool output includes pattern_analyst_insights — use trend_champion, seasonality_champion, and insights.recommended_method_hint to bias your candidate selection toward non-mean methods. MUST propose at least 3 candidates including at least 1 non-baseline type. Return ONLY JSON per instructions. IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY reference candidate names that appear in the tool output candidate_library; unknown names will hard-stop.
PATTERN ANALYST INSIGHTS: trend_champion='THETA', seasonality_champion='ARIMA', recommended_method='dba_combination'. Narrative: The analysis reveals significant model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.67) and high seasonality variance (1.41), with ARIMA dominating overall RMSE while THETA leads in trend tracking. Given both high disagreement and seasonality variance, DBA combination is recommended to leverage complementary strengths across models rather than relying on simple averaging or inverse-RMSE weighting.
prompts.skeptic:
Chame build_debate_packet_tool() PRIMEIRO (inputs via context). Depois retorne APENAS JSON (sem markdown) com add_names, remove_names, params_overrides, rationale, changes, when_good.
IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY use candidate names from valid_candidate_names; unknown names hard-stop.
You may only remove/override candidates that are in current_candidate_names (or candidates you are adding).

raw.pattern_analyst:
{
  "trend_champion": "THETA",
  "seasonality_champion": "ARIMA",
  "overall_champion": "ARIMA",
  "horizon_specialists": {"early": "ONLY_CWT_rf", "late": "ARIMA"},
  "tier1_models": ["ARIMA", "CWT_catboost", "FT_catboost", "FT_rf", "ETS", "ONLY_CWT_rf", "THETA", "ONLY_CWT_catboost"],
  "tier2_models": ["DWT_svr", "CWT_rf", "DWT_rf", "svr", "ONLY_DWT_rf", "NaiveMovingAverage", "DWT_catboost", "ONLY_FT_catboost"],
  "recommended_method_hint": "dba_combination",
  "recommended_weighting_basis": "mixed",
  "key_insights": {
    "rmse_spread_ratio": 0.6702,
    "high_disagreement": true,
    "high_seasonality_variance": true,
    "ytrue_trend_directions": ["down", "down", "down"]
  },
  "cot_narrative": "The analysis reveals significant model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.67) and high seasonality variance (1.41), with ARIMA dominating overall RMSE while THETA leads in trend tracking. Given both high disagreement and seasonality variance, DBA combination is recommended to leverage complementary strengths across models rather than relying on simple averaging or inverse-RMSE weighting."
}
raw.proposer:
{
  "selected_names": ["dba_combination", "best_per_horizon_by_validation", "inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35", "topk_mean_per_horizon_k5"],
  "params_overrides": {},
  "score_preset": "rmse_focus",
  "force_debate": false,
  "debate_margin": 0.02,
  "rationale": "n_unique_winners=10 indicates strong per-horizon model variation, favoring best_per_horizon_by_validation. relative_spread_mean=0.27 exceeds the 0.25 disagreement threshold, supporting dba_combination (explicitly recommended by pattern_analyst_insights). RMSE spread ratio=0.67 suggests top-k selection over full mean. inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35 provides balanced weighted averaging with shrinkage for stability."
}
raw.skeptic:
{
  "add_names": [],
  "remove_names": [],
  "params_overrides": {},
  "rationale": "Current candidate set has good diversity: 2 selection (best_per_horizon_by_validation, topk_mean_per_horizon_k5), 1 weighted (inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35), 1 baseline (dba_combination). No leakage detected - all use safe strategies (selection uses validation data, baseline has no fitting, weighted uses anti-leakage rolling/expanding selection). Candidate count is 4 with no single type dominating (>4 of same type). Universe leaderboards show top candidates but current set already includes best_per_horizon_by_validation and topk_mean_per_horizon_k5 which are in top rankings. No changes needed - set is diverse, non-leaking, and well-balanced.",
  "changes": [],
  "when_good": ""
}
raw.statistician: {"add_names": ["poly_weighted_average_p0.5_trim0.6", "robust_median"], "remove_names": [], "params_overrides": {}, "rationale": "1. poly_weighted_average_p0.5_trim0.6 has RMSE=178.48 in universe leaderboard, beating current best (178.58) by ~0.1. 2. Stability flag shows rmse_std_over_rmse=0.36>0.3 threshold - adding robust_median addresses this. 3. n_windows=3 is low, so poly_weighted_average with trim_ratio=0.6 provides appropriate regularization. Current set already has dba_combination and best_per_horizon_by_validation for high disagreement (relative_spread_mean=0.27≥0.25).", "changes": ["added poly_weighted_average_p0.5_trim0.6 for better RMSE from universe leaderboard", "added robust_median for stability issues"], "when_good": "When n_windows increases to ≥6, consider adding ridge_stacking methods. When rmse_std_over_rmse drops below 0.3, can reduce shrinkage on inverse_rmse_weights candidates."}

pattern_analyst.cot_narrative: The analysis reveals significant model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.67) and high seasonality variance (1.41), with ARIMA dominating overall RMSE while THETA leads in trend tracking. Given both high disagreement and seasonality variance, DBA combination is recommended to leverage complementary strengths across models rather than relying on simple averaging or inverse-RMSE weighting.

proposer.rationale: n_unique_winners=10 indicates strong per-horizon model variation, favoring best_per_horizon_by_validation. relative_spread_mean=0.27 exceeds the 0.25 disagreement threshold, supporting dba_combination (explicitly recommended by pattern_analyst_insights). RMSE spread ratio=0.67 suggests top-k selection over full mean. inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35 provides balanced weighted averaging with shrinkage for stability.

skeptic.rationale: Current candidate set has good diversity: 2 selection (best_per_horizon_by_validation, topk_mean_per_horizon_k5), 1 weighted (inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35), 1 baseline (dba_combination). No leakage detected - all use safe strategies (selection uses validation data, baseline has no fitting, weighted uses anti-leakage rolling/expanding selection). Candidate count is 4 with no single type dominating (>4 of same type). Universe leaderboards show top candidates but current set already includes best_per_horizon_by_validation and topk_mean_per_horizon_k5 which are in top rankings. No changes needed - set is diverse, non-leaking, and well-balanced.

statistician.rationale: 1. poly_weighted_average_p0.5_trim0.6 has RMSE=178.48 in universe leaderboard, beating current best (178.58) by ~0.1. 2. Stability flag shows rmse_std_over_rmse=0.36>0.3 threshold - adding robust_median addresses this. 3. n_windows=3 is low, so poly_weighted_average with trim_ratio=0.6 provides appropriate regularization. Current set already has dba_combination and best_per_horizon_by_validation for high disagreement (relative_spread_mean=0.27≥0.25).
changes: added poly_weighted_average_p0.5_trim0.6 for better RMSE from universe leaderboard
statistician.when_good: When n_windows increases to ≥6, consider adding ridge_stacking methods. When rmse_std_over_rmse drops below 0.3, can reduce shrinkage on inverse_rmse_weights candidates.
